steps.title: Set up the quadratic equation
steps.content:
Area = length × width, so:
$$186 = (6w - 5) \cdot w$$
Expanding:
$$186 = 6w^2 - 5w$$
Rearranging to standard form:
$$6w^2 - 5w - 186 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Look for factors in the form $(6w + a)(w + b)$.
We need $ab = -186$ and $6b + a = -5$.
Since $186 = 6 \times 31$, try $a = 31$ and $b = -6$:
$(6w + 31)(w - 6) = 6w^2 - 36w + 31w - 186 = 6w^2 - 5w - 186$ ✓

steps.title: Solve for width
steps.content:
Setting each factor to zero:
$6w + 31 = 0 \Rightarrow w = -\frac{31}{6}$ (reject: width cannot be negative)
$w - 6 = 0 \Rightarrow w = 6$
The width of the rectangle is $6$ inches.
